flashcards.question: End behavior of f(x) = -2x⁴ + 5x²
flashcards.answer: As x→∞, f→-∞; as x→-∞, f→-∞. Even degree, negative leading coeff.

flashcards.question: Multiplicity 3 zero at x = 2 means what?
flashcards.answer: Graph crosses at x=2 with inflection point. Odd multiplicity = crosses.

flashcards.question: Even multiplicity zero behavior
flashcards.answer: Graph touches but doesn't cross x-axis (bounce). Local extremum.

flashcards.question: Sketch f(x) = (x + 1)²(x - 2). Zeros, multiplicities, end behavior?
flashcards.answer: Zeros: -1 (mult. 2, touches), 2 (mult. 1, crosses). Opens up (positive leading coeff, degree 3).

flashcards.question: Number of real zeros: degree 5 polynomial
flashcards.answer: Can have 1, 3, or 5 real zeros. Complex zeros come in pairs.

flashcards.question: Local maximum/minimum occur at zeros of what?
flashcards.answer: Zeros of derivative f'(x). Critical points.

flashcards.question: If zero has multiplicity 4, touching or crossing?
flashcards.answer: Touching (even multiplicity). Local extremum, doesn't cross.
